rounds.agent_message: Seed round copied from the highest existing source round.

Implemented an MFMA-based substrate kernel in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p14/optimization_rounds_no_invariants/round1/output_model_new.py) and kept [candidate_input.py](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p14/optimization_rounds_no_invariants/round1/candidate_input.py) unchanged. The kernel now compiles and runs through the round harness, but it does not pass correctness yet. The latest round-local evaluation in [debug_eval.jsonl](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p14/optimization_rounds_no_invariants/round1/debug_eval.jsonl) reports `compiled: true` and `correctness: false` with output mismatch. The remaining issue is the exact MFMA operand/writeback lane mapping for `mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32`; I was able to get the required primitives issued, but not the final numerically correct layout within the allowed round time.

rounds.agent_message: Implemented a new MFMA/LDS path in [output_model_new.py](/workspace/kb_eval_pipeline/runs/kb1/p14/optimization_rounds_no_invariants/round2/output_model_new.py), centered on `raw_buffer_load_x4`, LDS staging in `u32` words, and BF16 operand reinterprets for `mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32`. The last command run was the exact required round-local eval command, and it exited with that lowering error rather than a correctness result.
